title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Repository Operations Board
summary: Operate an unfamiliar simulated Git repository across worktree, index, history, branches, remotes, conflicts, content policy, releases, revision queries, recovery, rewriting, gates, and collaboration without workshop solutions. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.
